target.title: fix(agents): retry transient provider errors when single profile has no fallback
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #84236 by adding a bounded same-profile retry for transient provider overloads when an embedded-agent run has only one auth profile and no fallback model/profile available.

- Fix classification: root-cause bug fix for the embedded-agent provider failover decision contract.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: high; the change is bounded, covered by focused tests, and revalidated with current-head real behavior proof.
- Why it matters / User impact: a temporary provider overload could make a single-profile agent stop instead of retrying the primary provider once, which hurts users who only have one configured provider/profile.
- What did NOT change: no retry is added for rate limits, auth failures, billing failures, non-overload provider errors, configured fallback model/profile routing, provider selection, public config schema, or unrelated runner behavior.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: the source of truth is the normalized embedded-runner `FailoverReason` and failover policy, not provider-specific MiniMax string matching; this PR keeps the default/fallback contract unchanged except for the overloaded-only no-fallback retry.
- Related open PR / same-contract scan: current `origin/main` has overlapping embedded-runner failover edits, but it does not cover this single-profile no-fallback MiniMax overload retry behavior; this PR remains the focused same-contract fix for #84236.
- Patch quality notes: patch-quality retry/default warnings are expected because this bug fix deliberately adds one bounded retry; the guard is explicit (`overloaded` only, no fallback only, one retry only, 1000ms floor) so the change is not a broad fallback or symptom-masking workaround.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: the embedded-agent failover state machine already had source-of-truth routing for fallback/profile rotation, but the single-profile no-fallback `overloaded` branch had no bounded retry state, causing the normalized transient overload reason to exit the run instead of exercising the same provider once.
- Why this is root-cause fix: the patch changes the failover decision invariant itself for both prompt-side and assistant-side paths: when the normalized reason is `overloaded` and no fallback exists, the state machine enters exactly one same-profile transient retry before surfacing the canonical overloaded 503. It does not hide errors downstream or special-case MiniMax text.

## What changed

- Add an overloaded-only same-profile retry policy for no-fallback embedded-agent runs.
- Apply the policy on both prompt-side and assistant-side failover paths.
- Preserve existing behavior for non-overload reasons: rate limits, auth failures, billing failures, and other provider errors do not get same-profile retries.
- Record same-profile retry attempts in the runner trace.
- Cover the new behavior with focused failover-policy, assistant-failover, and auth-profile-rotation tests.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: issue #84236's exact MiniMax overloaded failure shape in a single-profile no-fallback embedded-agent run.
- Real environment tested: current PR worktree at `08303def2f00b45ea787453eaece5c7576d559db`, rebased on `origin/main` `848f39e70d003c4060e2474874e7b33890362884`; local OpenClaw dev gateway startup with `deepseek/deepseek-chat`; production `handleAssistantFailover` decision function for the MiniMax overload path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: started the OpenClaw dev gateway from this worktree on port 19184, then ran the current-head decision proof script that feeds `handleAssistantFailover` with provider `minimax`, model `MiniMax-M2.7`, `overloaded_error`, "server is busy, please retry later", single profile, and no fallback.
- Evidence after fix: `openclaw-pr-84265-evidence/dev-gateway-19184.log`, `openclaw-pr-84265-evidence/same-profile-overload-proof-current.log`, and `openclaw-pr-84265-evidence/verification-summary-current.md`.
- Observed result after fix: gateway reached `ready`; first MiniMax overloaded failure returns one `same_profile_transient` retry with a 1000ms backoff; retry exhaustion surfaces overloaded 503 for the same provider/model without looping.
- What was not tested: no live MiniMax outage was triggered; the proof uses the production failover decision function with the exact error shape from the issue, plus a real local gateway startup, rather than attempting to induce a provider-side outage.

## Verification

- Target test file: `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/failover-policy.test.ts`, and `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.run-embedded-agent.auth-profile-rotation.e2e.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: MiniMax-style `overloaded_error` / "server is busy, please retry later" maps to normalized `overloaded`; single-profile no-fallback runs retry the same provider/model once, then surface overloaded 503 after the cap; rate-limit and auth failures do not use this same-profile retry path.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: helper tests lock the production failover decision without cold-loading the whole runner, while the auth-profile-rotation e2e test proves the runner trace/state integration for the no-fallback same-profile retry.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/failover-policy.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.run-embedded-agent.auth-profile-rotation.e2e.test.ts`.
- Observed result after fix: 81 tests passed across 3 shards.
- Evidence after fix: `openclaw-pr-84265-evidence/touched-tests-current.log`.
- Diff hygiene: `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed.

## Regression Test Plan

- Focused helper coverage proves the assistant-side no-fallback overloaded retry, retry-cap exhaustion, and non-overloaded exclusions.
- Failover policy coverage proves same-profile retry eligibility is `overloaded` only.
- Auth-profile rotation e2e coverage proves the runner records `same_profile_retry` and does not rotate to a nonexistent profile for this no-fallback transient overload path.

## Review findings addressed

- RF-001: maintainer-decision / disclosed. This intentionally changes the default no-fallback overloaded path from immediate surfacing to one extra same-profile provider call plus at least 1s backoff.
- RF-002: maintainer-decision / disclosed. The retry is deliberately limited to `failoverReason === "overloaded"`; rate limit, auth, billing, and other provider failures keep their existing surface/fallback behavior.
- RF-003: maintainer-decision / disclosed. A persistent provider overload can occupy the run slightly longer before surfacing the same overloaded 503, because the retry is capped at one attempt with a 1000ms minimum backoff.
- RF-004: no code action needed. The current diff is the minimal canonical fix for the reported single-profile MiniMax overload freeze; broader configurable retry policy, per-provider retry counts, or retrying other provider errors are out of scope.
- Real behavior proof request: addressed with current-head gateway startup proof plus production decision-function proof using the exact issue #84236 MiniMax overloaded error shape.

## CI attribution

The visible failing checks were on the previous PR head `06f14170...`, before this branch was synced to current `origin/main`.

- `build-artifacts`: failed in `test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts`, which this PR does not touch. The same failure reproduces on a pristine `origin/main` worktree, so this is a main-baseline/environment failure rather than a PR regression.
- `checks-node-agentic-commands-doctor`, `checks-node-agentic-command-support`, and `checks-node-core-runtime-infra-state`: all exited with code 143 (SIGTERM), consistent with runner termination/timeout/cancellation rather than assertion failures. These shards also do not run the touched embedded-agent tests.
- The PR's touched tests run in the embedded-agent runner shards and pass locally on the current head.

## Merge risk

- Risk labels considered: `merge-risk: compatibility`, `merge-risk: auth-provider`, and `merge-risk: availability`.
- Risk explanation: the default no-fallback overloaded path changes from immediate surfacing to one extra same-profile provider call plus at least 1s backoff; a persistent provider overload can therefore take slightly longer before surfacing overloaded 503.
- Why acceptable: the behavior is limited to `failoverReason === "overloaded"`, only when no fallback profile/model is configured, capped at one retry, and leaves rate limit/auth/billing/other provider failures unchanged.
